subject: wolves
one_liner: Checking, savings, and carcass escrow for the modern pack, behind one login your alpha controls.
brand_voice_notes: Private-bank calm. Mercury cadence: short declarative claims, zero exclamation points, compliance language played completely straight. The joke is that pack hierarchy maps perfectly onto corporate treasury controls and nobody at the company has questioned the premise. Money is denominated in elk. The regulatory footer is load-bearing.

hero.sub: Denhold gives your pack checking, savings, and carcass escrow behind one login. Open an account in minutes. Final approval rests with your alpha, as it should.

features.title: Alpha-approval workflows
features.blurb: Every withdrawal over one (1) elk routes to your alpha for sign-off. Disputes are resolved the traditional way, and the outcome is written back to the ledger automatically.

features.title: Kill escrow
features.blurb: Funds release only after the carcass is verified on-site by two independent pack members. 94% of escrows clear before the ravens arrive. The other 6% become a raven problem, which is outside our terms of service.

features.title: Territory expense tracking
features.blurb: Scent-marking runs are categorized automatically and reconciled against your territory's forty-square-mile budget. Overspray appears on the map in amber, and someone will be asked about it at the next moon review.

pricing_tiers.name: Lone Wolf
pricing_tiers.tagline: For dispersers building something of their own.
perks: No approval workflows (there is no one to ask)
One complimentary cache audit per winter

pricing_tiers.name: Apex
perks: Succession planning, for when leadership changes suddenly

testimonials.quote: We moved our entire elk position into Denhold in one afternoon. The ravens noticed nothing.
testimonials.name: Asena
testimonials.title: Beta & Head of Treasury, Lamar Canyon Pack

testimonials.quote: Before Denhold, our caches were spread across nine locations and nobody remembered six of them. Now it's four.
testimonials.name: Grey
testimonials.title: Chief Foraging Officer, Sawtooth Pack

testimonials.quote: The alpha-approval flow ended three fights a week. It started one, but the ledger says it was justified.
testimonials.name: Two Socks
testimonials.title: Dispersal-Stage Associate, formerly of the Druid Peak Pack

demo_screen_concept: A dark Mercury-style banking dashboard for the Lamar Canyon Pack operating account: headline balance denominated in ELK with a pounds-of-meat conversion beneath it, a transactions table mixing inflows (bull elk intercept, north meadow) with outflows (cache withdrawal, a disputed carcass charge attributed to ravens), an interactive Approvals queue where the viewer acts as alpha and approves or denies pending withdrawals, a moon-cycle statement selector, and a territory spend meter that ticks toward its forty-square-mile budget.

images.rationale: hero skipped: cartographic/typographic direction carries the hero with contour lines and an account-snapshot plate, a photo would fight the map conceit; demo assets skipped: demo_screen_concept is a banking dashboard, not photo-shaped; full budget on three testimonial headshots, the always-applicable slot, shot as deadpan corporate portraits on the spruce studio backdrop

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a grey wolf, shoulders squared to camera like a LinkedIn profile photo, calm confident expression, adult female timber wolf with silver-grey coat, soft professional studio lighting, dark spruce-green studio backdrop (deep green-black #0F1713), muted sage-green tones #AFC2B2, editorial photograph, one focal subject, shallow depth of field. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of an older grizzled grey wolf with pale grey and white fur, distinguished senior-executive bearing, slight jowls, direct gaze at camera like an annual-report portrait, soft professional studio lighting, dark spruce-green studio backdrop (deep green-black #0F1713), muted sage-green tones #AFC2B2, editorial photograph, one focal subject, shallow depth of field. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a young lanky grey wolf with dark charcoal fur and slightly awkward earnest expression, junior-associate energy, ears a bit too big, photographed like a company directory photo, soft professional studio lighting, dark spruce-green studio backdrop (deep green-black #0F1713), muted sage-green tones #AFC2B2, editorial photograph, one focal subject, shallow depth of field. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Cartographic field-atlas private bank, moonlit-territory mode. The page is staged as the client portal of a discreet wilderness bank whose branches are dens and whose statements follow the lunar cycle. A deep spruce-black ground carries faint CSS topographic contour lines drifting behind the hero and hairline map-grid ticks along section edges, so the whole page reads like a surveyed territory rather than a website. Panels are flat map plates: 1px moon-sage hairline borders, 6px radii, small corner coordinates set in mono, no glass, no blur, no offset shadows. One moon-sage silver-green carries the wordmark, headlines, structural hairlines, and every institutional surface; one rationed eyeshine amber is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA, the pending-approval affordance, and overspray warnings on the territory meter, and appears almost nowhere else. Marcellus sets the wordmark and headlines in inscriptional Roman caps with the gravity of a bronze trailhead plaque that happens to hold your deposits; Instrument Sans carries calm, procedural body copy. Every figure that pretends to be treasury data (ELK balances, escrow percentages, tier prices, cache counts, moon dates) is set in DM Mono with tabular figures inside plate chips so it reads like survey data, not marketing. Motion is nocturnal and slow: contour lines drift almost imperceptibly, the moon-phase glyph in the nav ticks through its cycle on an 8s loop, plates rise 6px and fade in on scroll, the CTA presses down 1px, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No light mode, no neon, no grain, no bento lattice, no marquee bulbs. Reads like a bank that considers a wolf pack a straightforward corporate structure, because structurally it is one.

fonts.body: 'Instrument Sans', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Private-bank fintech landing, Mercury-adjacent: slim wordmark nav with moon-phase glyph, split hero (inscriptional serif claim left, operating-account snapshot plate right), features as three surveyed map plates, testimonials as field notes with headshots, pricing as membership tiers, and a regulatory footer stating that Denhold is a financial technology company, not a bank.
design_direction.motion_vocab: drifting contour lines; moon-phase glyph ticking through its cycle; plates rising 6px on scroll; balance figures settling with a brief tick; CTA pressing down 1px; all behind prefers-reduced-motion
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Split hero on the spruce ground with contour lines strongest here. Left column: Marcellus h1 sized large, procedural sub, one amber CTA and one hairline secondary link. Right column: an account snapshot plate for the Lamar Canyon Pack with corner coordinates, an ELK balance in DM Mono, a pending alpha-approval row glowing faint amber, and a moon-cycle statement date.
